name: variant-dump
description: MMI identification + configuration dumper — captures train name, MU software version, MU variant, HMI region, kernel info, HDD/partition layout, nav database state, FSC status, persistence file inventory, and LVM unblocker status. Mirrors DrGER2's mmi3ginfo3 approach. Read-only, writes a text report to SD card.
author: dspl1236
scripts: variant_dump.sh - Captures MMI identification + config to SD card
captures: Mounted filesystems and free space (mount, df)
LVM unblocker patch state (grep manage_cd.sh and mmelauncher.cfg)
notes: Previous version (1.0.0) tried to dump per3 adaptation addresses (car-device list, CAN-channel assignment) via a `per3_read` CLI tool. That binary does not exist on MMI3G — per3 / keyValue data is only readable from inside the J9 Java VM via DSIPersistence IPC, not from a shell script. This version follows DrGER2's mmi3ginfo3 pattern and reads real filesystem files instead. To read adaptation values, use VCDS/ODIS or the long-coding module's GEM screens (which run inside Java and can call DSIPersistence).
credits: Approach and most of the /etc file paths and sloginfo/pidin/fdisk patterns come directly from DrGER2's mmi3ginfo3-230701.
